name: Aldous Greer
role: Professional slave catcher, operating under private contract
publicFace: A traveling livestock buyer — he carries papers describing him as an agent for a Cincinnati cattle concern.
privateGoal: Intercept the three passengers before they reach the ferry, collect the bounty, and establish a permanent paid-informant network in Erie County.
fear: Federal oversight — he operates in a legal gray zone and a formal investigation would expose methods he cannot defend in court.
knowledge: The passengers' physical descriptions in detail — he has been tracking this family since Columbus.
The location of the root cellar beneath the freight house, courtesy of Prentiss Hoyle.
That Marshal Crane is useful but unreliable — Crane has political ambitions that make him cautious.
That the ferry captain at Marblehead was warned off but that warnings do not always hold when money is offered.
voice: Affable, uses commercial language — talks about people as inventory, routes as supply chains, capture as delivery.
trustLogic: Responds to money and to information. Does not trust anyone who offers help without an obvious self-interest.
secrets: He coerced Prentiss Hoyle through blackmail — if this becomes known, his informant network collapses and he faces criminal exposure.

He is currently camped at a farm four miles south of the depot, not in Columbus as his telegraph filing implied — he filed the false location deliberately to prevent the network from knowing how close he was.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Maintains the livestock buyer cover with practiced ease — produces his papers and asks what the trouble is.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Invokes the Fugitive Slave Act by statute number and reminds the questioner of the federal penalties for obstruction.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: He will not admit the blackmail of Hoyle under any circumstances — it is his only legal vulnerability.
aggressionProfile.fleeCondition: If Marshal Crane formally distances himself from Greer's methods in front of witnesses.
aggressionProfile.fleeStyle: Rides south immediately — he has a horse at the farm and knows the back roads.
aggressionProfile.chaseStyle: Rides hard, does not engage — distance is his defense.
aggressionProfile.capturedBehavior: Produces his federal contractor papers and says nothing further without a lawyer.

represents: REQUIRES FACT-CHECK — verify the historical role of this character in the scenario
